summary: Document recommended GitHub branch-protection settings for main
description:
Branch protection is the half of the PR workflow that doesn't live in
the tool — it's GitHub settings. Without it, agents can still push
directly to main and bypass the whole point of switching to PRs.

Add a short section to BIBLE.md (right after 'The Branch Workflow')
recommending:
- Require pull request before merging
- Require status checks to pass (the existing 'Lint, typecheck, test' job)
- Require linear history (no squash, per the existing skill's --no-ff rule)
- (Optional) Require at least 1 approval — the user approving their own
  agents' PRs

Keep it tight (~15 lines). Reference 'todo pr' as the command that
opens PRs once that ticket ships.

Done contract: a 'Branch Protection (recommended)' subsection in BIBLE.md
with the above settings. No code change.

resolution.note: Added a 'Branch Protection (recommended)' subsection to BIBLE.md covering the four GitHub settings that make the trunk-based PR flow safe for agent-driven work.
